Contractor Terms and Assignment Agreement

This Agreement is made on [Agreement Date] between:

Client / Landlord [Landlord or Company Name]
Registered Address [Landlord Address]
Contractor [Contractor Full Name or Business Name]
Contractor Address [Contractor Address]
Property / Job Reference [Property Address or Job Reference]

1. Appointment

The Client appoints the Contractor to carry out the services described in this Agreement and any work order, quote approval, or written instruction issued through Tenaqo or any agreed written communication channel.

2. Scope of Services

The Contractor shall carry out maintenance, repair, installation, inspection, or other agreed property services with reasonable care and skill and in accordance with all applicable laws, regulations, safety requirements, and manufacturer guidance.

3. Standard of Work

  1. All work must be completed to a professional standard.
  2. The Contractor shall keep the property reasonably clean and safe during the works.
  3. The Contractor shall promptly report any unexpected issues, delays, risks, or additional works.
  4. The Contractor shall not subcontract the works without the Client’s prior written approval.

4. Access and Occupiers

  1. The Contractor shall act courteously and professionally when dealing with tenants, residents, and staff.
  2. The Contractor shall only attend at agreed times unless dealing with an emergency callout.
  3. The Contractor shall comply with access instructions and safeguarding requirements notified by the Client.

5. Insurance and Compliance

  1. The Contractor confirms they hold all licences, registrations, and certifications needed for the work.
  2. The Contractor shall maintain suitable public liability insurance and any legally required trade insurance.
  3. The Contractor shall provide evidence of insurance or certification when requested.

6. Price and Payment

  1. Charges shall follow the approved quote, agreed schedule of rates, or written instruction.
  2. No material variation or extra work shall be charged without prior approval unless emergency conditions make that impossible.
  3. Invoices must clearly identify the property, job reference, date of work, and agreed charges.
  4. Payment terms: [e.g. 14 days from approved invoice].

7. Defects and Callbacks

The Contractor shall return within a reasonable time to correct defects or incomplete work attributable to the Contractor’s workmanship or materials, at no additional charge unless otherwise agreed.

8. Health, Safety, and Reporting

  1. The Contractor shall comply with all applicable health and safety obligations.
  2. The Contractor shall stop work and notify the Client if unsafe conditions arise.
  3. The Contractor shall immediately report accidents, damage, near misses, or safeguarding concerns.

9. Data, Confidentiality, and Records

  1. The Contractor shall keep tenant, property, and account information confidential.
  2. The Contractor shall use personal data only as needed to perform the work.
  3. The Contractor shall not retain or disclose confidential information except where legally required.

10. Term and Termination

  1. This Agreement begins on the date above and continues until terminated by either party.
  2. Either party may terminate for material breach, insolvency, or unlawful conduct.
  3. The Client may end a specific assignment or work order at any time, paying only for properly completed work up to termination.

11. Liability

Nothing in this Agreement excludes liability that cannot legally be excluded. Subject to that, each party’s liability shall be limited to reasonably foreseeable direct losses arising from breach of this Agreement.

12. Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, unless the parties expressly agree a different governing law in writing.

Signed for the Client Name: [Client Signatory Name]
Title: [Title]
Date: [Date] 		Signed by the Contractor Name: [Contractor Signatory Name]
Business Name: [Business Name]
Date: [Date]
Usage note: Replace the bracketed placeholders before upload, or leave the names and dates for provider fields. In DocuSeal or DocuSign, add signature fields and date fields on the two signature blocks.

This template is a practical starting point for operational use and should be reviewed for your jurisdiction, insurance requirements, and commercial terms before production use.
